A soubriquet of officers of the Bengal Presidency. na koī , Nobody; no one; none. Na-koī ātā thā ghar meṅ, na-koī jātā thā, Na-koī god meṅ le-kar mujhe sulātā thā . Nobody came to the house, nobody from it went, Nobody in his lap fed me, nor me to sleep e'er sent. ☞ During the absence of her husband who was abroad a stranger was a frequent visitor of the wife's. more: adj. S. केवल 1. Blank ( sādā , 1). 2. New; unused. 3. Unbleached (cloth, etc.). 4. Untutored; untaught. Chār baras maktab meṅ paṛhe, par kore hī rahe . 5. Simple; stupid. Tum bhī kore chālīs sere ho! Prov. Well! you are a fool full standard weight! 6. ( koram-kār ) Poor; penniless; destitute. korā , adv. Only (خالي 1). Ek billī, ek baglā dekhā, oṛh faqīrī khilkā, Bāhar se sab gyān ko chhāṅṭeṅ, andar korā chhilkā, Koī safā na dekhā dil kā! Bhajan. Kabīr. In friar's robes a cat and eke a crane I saw, without they seemed all truth to know, within mere straw: none pure in heart I saw! korā bachnā , v. n. To escape unhurt; come off with a whole skin. korā bartan , n. 1. A new vessel. 2. ( Met .) A maid; virgin. Tāzgī jī kī aur tarī tan kī, Vāh! kyā bāt kore bartan kī! Double entendre. Nazīr. The frame refreshed, the heart with new life fed, Such zest in vessels new unlimited! korā-pan , n. m.…
n. m. 1. Sweepings; rubbish (آخور). 2. Share; lot. kūṛ'ī, kuṛṛī, kur'aṛ , W.; ginaurā , E. n. f. 1. A dunghill. 2. Dust, ash, or dungheaps. 3. Heaps of refuse for manure.

n. m. Dim. kauṛī , n. f. S. कपर्द्द A shell. kauṛī kos dauṛnā , To labor hard to earn a livelihood. Kauṛī kos dauṛ-kar peṭ bhartā hai . I have to run a penny a mile to get a belly full. kauṛe , n. m. 1. Money; wealth. 2. Price. Kauṛe haiṅ is aṅgarkhe ke! Delhī Street cry. kauṛe k. , v. n. 1. To sell off; sell cheap. 2. To turn into cash; to realize.

adj. New, unhandled, unused, fresh (applied chiefly to earthen vessels, clothes, or paper). کوري رهنا kore rahnā, To be disappointed.
adj. (dakh.) Askew, askaunt ( Harris ). کوري آنکهـ سين ديکهنا korī ānkh seṅ dekhnā, To look askew or askaunt, to leer.

s.m. 'An undefiled body'; an unmarried person; a bachelor; a virgin or spinster; — maidenhood, virginity

v.n. To remain unprofited or unsatisfied; to get nothing, to be disappointed.

or koṛā mārnā (-ko), To apply the lash to, to lash, whip, flog.

def: s f. Cost of digging out, &c.
n. f. The cost of digging.
s.m. A bud, blossom, an unblown flower.
s.m. The sacred book of the Muḥammadans, the Qorʼān (or Korān, Coran, or Alcoran)
more: n. m. قرا reading. The sacred book of the Mahomedans. It consists of revelations made to Mahomed, and delivered by him orally, and collected and committed to writing by the Khalif Omar. 1. Panḍit pothī bāṅchte, mullāṅ paṛhe qurān, Log dikhāvā lākh paḍho, nānh mile Bhagvān . The Pandit Shāstars reads, the Mulla his Quran for show, But let them spout on e'er so much their God is not found so. 2. Qurān par qurān rakhne kā kyā muzāëqā . Prov. Pile on Quran upon Quran, what matters it? (No other thing should be placed on the sacred book). qurān uṭhānā, yā sir par rakhnā, qurān kī qasam khānā, qurān par hāth rakhnā, yā dharnā , v. a. To take an oath on the Qurān ; to kiss the book. Phr. So help me God. Jis bāt kī chāho qasam ek martaba le lo! Har bār to Qurān uṭhāyā nahīṅ jātā . Rind. Once as you will upon the sacred book I'll swear, But constantly the book take up I cannot dare. qurān ṭhanḍā …
subs: To raise the Qorʼān (to swear), to take an oath on the Qorʼān
To place the hand on the Qorʼān (to swear by), to swear on the Qorʼān
To let the Qorʼān fall or drop (he who drops the Qorʼān must give its weight of corn as alms to the poor)
s.m. A reader of the Qorʼān; — a person deposed from rule or dignity, one who is dismissed from office, &c.
'To place the Qorʼān on the head,' to swear on the Qorʼān
'To wear a garment made of the leaves of the Qorʼān'; to become the Qorʼān itself; — to swear excessively; to take an oath on the most trivial occasion
To go or read through the Qorʼān; to repeat the Qorʼān (to one another, two or more people).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قرآن (qur'ān), from Arabic اَلْقُرْآن (al-qurʔān). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi कुरांन (kurāṃna /⁠kurā̃n⁠/) / قرآن (qrān /⁠qurān⁠/).

def: v.t. To cause to be hollowed out, to get (a thing, &c.) scooped or dug out; to have (a thing) carved or engraved.

A short life. kotāh-qad , adj. Of short stature ( pastah-qad ). kotāh k. , To settle ( chukānā , 4, 5). kotāh-gardan , adj. 1. Short-necked. 2. ( Met .) Deceitful; wicked.

adj. 1. Short, little, small, brief. 2. Mean. کوتاه بين kotāh-bīn, Dim-sighted, shortsighted. کوتاه بيني kotāh-bīn-ī, s. f. Shortsightedness. کوتاه پاچهہ kotāh-pācha, Name of a quadruped with a spotted back and with horns like a stag. کوتاه دست kotāh-dast, Shorthanded; weak. کوتاه نظر kotāh-naz̤ar, adj. Shortsighted.
subs: or kotāh-pāʼe, s.m. lit. 'Short-footed'; a kind of quadruped with a spotted back and horns like a deer (the term is applied sometimes to a hare); (met.) a very short sneaking little man
adj. Short-handed; slow, backward, not forward or prone (e.g. to punish, or to tyrannize); weak
v.t. To cut short, to shorten, abridge; to settle; to hold short (of, -se), to withhold (from), to be slow or backward (in)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کوتاه.

def: adj. contrac. of kotāh (q.v. for compounds).
more: adj. contract. of کوتاه q. v. کوته انديش kotah-andesh, Shortsighted, imprudent, improvident, indiscreet, wanting foresight. کوته انديشي kotah-andesh-ī, s. f. Shortsightedness, imprudence, indiscretion, improvidence. کوته قد kotah-ḳadd, Of short stature. کوته گردن kotah-gardan, Short-necked: ( met. ) Wicked, deceitful.

ety: kotāh, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Smallness, shortness; brevity; deficiency, failure, lack, want (syn. kamī, q.v.); narrowness; littleness, meanness
more: s. f. 1. Smallness, shortness, brevity. 2. Meanness, littleness. 3. Deficiency.
subs: (-meṅ), To fail, fall short (of), be lacking (in), &c. (see kamī karnā).

def: n. f. See کمي 1. Want; lack. 2. Narrowness. kotāhī k. , v. a. See kamī k .
s.m. contrac. of kotāhī, q.v.
more: n. f. Pālī, koṭṭham . 1. A masonry house; a mansion. 2. A storehouse; granary; barn. Ghar bār sab tumhārā hai, par koṭhī kuṭhle ke hāth na lagānā . Prov. The house and all in it is yours, but lay not hands on larder or on barn. (A satire on the empty Native courtesy of saying to the visitor or guest 'this house is yours'). 3. A factory. Nīl kī koṭhī . An Indigo factory. 4. A banking house; a bank. 5. ( Panj. chhallā koṭhī ) A house of call; a bawdy house. 6. The womb. 7. The shaft of a well or mine; a curb. 8. The chamber of a gun. 9. The ferule or mounting of a scabbard. koṭhī baiṭhnā , v. n. 1. See divālā nikalnā . 2. To be fixed in its place (the shaft of a well). Wāṅ koṭhī koṭhī baiṭh gaī, yāṅ khetī bāṛī khet rahī . Nazīr. There fallen houses meet your eye, here fields untilled neglected lie. koṭhī se hisāb kitāb rakhnā , v. a. To deal with a bank. koṭhī k., yā kholnā , v. a. To open…

def: n. Cor. of Quarter guard.
s.m. A led horse, a second or spare horse led before a grandee (for show, or for use in case of emergency); — anything extra or superfluous
more: n. m. A horse led or kept for show.
s. m. A ledhorse. کوتل کش kotal-kash, s. m. A surcingle, particularly for fastening body-clothes.
subs: s.m. A surcingle (particularly for fastening body-clothes).

def: s. (dakh.) A mallet (carpenterʼs: Harris ).
n. f. A carpenter's mallet. (?)
s.m. Hollow (of a tree); hole made by a bird in a tree; a cavity.
more: s. m. A kind of greens.
s. m. Holes made by birds in trees.

def: s.m. A kind of greens or vegetable.
n. m. A hollow in a tree.
s.f. A naked woman; a form of the goddess Durgā (represented naked).
wety: From Sindhi ڪوٽڙي (koṭṛī), Ultimately from Sanskrit कोट (koṭa).

def: (see کوٿنا) adj. (dakh.) See لت خور lat-ḵẖor under لت lat.
s.m. Putrefaction, corruption; a sore; gangrene; inflammation and ulceration of the angles of the eyelids.
more: n. m. Corn in the ear.

def: s.m. (dialec.) Corn in the ear; an ear of corn (cf. ḵẖosha).

ety: Prk. कुट्ठो or कोट्ठो; S. कुष्ठः
def: s.m. A species of leprosy with large round spots; ringworm, impetigo.

s.m. A granary, barn; storehouse; — a house built of burnt brick or stone; a mercantile house or firm ( = koṭhī, which is the com. form); a large (inner) room or apartment; the flat top or roof of a house; an upper storey; a storey, floor, flat; — the upper half of the body; the stomach, abdomen, bowels; the womb; — a column (in a table, or form, &c.; syn. ḵẖāna); — the multiplication table (syn. pahāṛā)
more: s. m. A house built of burnt bricks or stone, a story, a floor.
s. m. (dakh.) A barn, granary, garner, cow-house ( Harris ).
s. (dakh.) Scale of a fish ( Harris ).
subs: The stomach, or bowels, or the womb, to become deranged
To sit on the house-top, or in an upper storey; — to become a prostitute
adj. Having constipated bowels, of constipated habit
To put away in a storehouse; to store up
or koṭhe-wālī, s.f. A public prostitute (opp. to ḵẖāṅgī).

def: n. m. S. कोष्ठ apartment. 1. A house built of bricks or stone. 2. A storehouse; granary; barn. 3. The upper story of a house ( bālā-khāna ). 4. An inner apartment. 5. The belly; stomach. 6. A column ( khānā , 6). 7. The multiplication table ( pahāṛā ). koṭhā bigaṛnā , v. n. To be deranged (the stomach or womb). koṭhe par baiṭhnā , v. n. lit . to sit in the upper story. To become a prostitute. Tujh ko nahīṅ lihāz to kab mujh ko sharm hai, Baiṭhūṅgī koṭhā le-ke maiṅ Bhinḍī bazār meṅ . Wom. If you 've no delicacy why should I feel shame? In open balcony I'll sit, my trade proclaim. koṭhe meṅ ḍālnā , v. a. To store up. koṭhe-vāliyāṅ , n. f. Public prostitutes, as distinguished from khāngī , q. v.
s.f. (dialec.) = koṭhī, q.v.

s.f. A granary, a barn; a large vessel of unbaked earth, or a large oblong bamboo basket (in which grain is kept), a bin, chest; a storehouse; warehouse; a factory; a treasury; banking-house, bank; a mercantile house or firm; a house built of brick or stone, mansion, dwelling, residence; a chamber, compartment, cell (of the body, &c.); the womb; hold or capacity (of a ship, or cart, &c.); chamber (of a gun, or a pipe, &c.); ferule or mounting (at the end of a scabbard); a circular piece of wood placed at the bottom of a well (to prevent the sand from moving, or as a foundation for the masonry lining), the inside shaft (of a well), a curb
more: s. f. 1. A small ( pakkā ) house, a warehouse, a granary, dwelling, mansion, barrack, chamber, bank, chest, bin, storehouse, factory. 2. A banking house. 3. The chamber of a gun, the inside or shaft of a well. 4. The womb. 5. The ferrule or mounting at the end of a scabbard. کوٿهي وال koṭhī-wāl, A wholesale merchant, a warehouse-keeper.
subs: A house to fall in; a mercantile house, or a bank, to fail; the shaft (of a well) to be fixed in its place
or koṭhī kholnā. To set up or start a factory, or a mercantile business; to open a bank
To sink a shaft, or a curb (in a well)
s.m. A warehouse keeper; a wholesale merchant; a banker.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A bag; a sack; a large earthen case or enclosure, or a bin of matting and wickerwork (to hold grain); a pocket; the chamber of the stomach, the stomach; a case to be stuffed (as a pillow).
more: n. m. 1. A bag. 2. A pocket. 3. The stomach; belly. kothlā bharnā , v. a. To fill the stomach. kothlī , n. f. 1. A small bag. 2. A bag of sweetmeats sent as a present.

def: s.m. = H کوٿهلي कोठली koṭhlī, s.f. dialec. forms of koṭhṛī, q.v.

ety: the preceding + S. इका for कः
def: s.f. (dim.) A small bag; a purse, &c. ( = kothlā); — a bag of sweetmeats (sent as a present).
s.f. = H کوٿهلا कोठला koṭhlā, s.m. dialec. forms of koṭhṛī, q.v.
s.f.m.(?) Coriander plant and seed, Coriandrum sativum (cf. dhaniyā).
more: n. m. Cor. of P. kashnīz . Coriander; Coriander sativum .
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀣𑀼𑀁𑀪𑀭𑀻 (kutthuṃbharī), from Sanskrit कुस्तुम्बरी (kustumbarī).

def: s.m. A (green) flying bug (so called because it has the smell of coriander).
s.f. 'A small house'; room or chamber (of a house), apartment, closet; a store-room; a granary; a division in a granary (for different kinds of grain).
more: s. f. A room, chamber, apartment, closet.

def: s.f. 'A small house'; room or chamber (of a house), apartment, closet; a store-room; a granary; a division in a granary (for different kinds of grain).
n. f. A small room or chamber; a closet; apartment; cabin.
s.m. (Braj) corr. of kotwāl, q.v. ( = S. कोट or कोष्ठ + पाल), s.m. lit. 'The keeper or porter of a castle'; the chief officer of the police for a city or town; city magistrate.
s.f. End, or top (of anything); edge, point (of a sword); horn (of the moon); point, eminence; side (of a right-augled triangle); — a crore, ten millions ( = kaṛoṛ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑁄𑀝𑁆𑀝 (koṭṭa), from Sanskrit कोट्ट (koṭṭa), कोट (koṭa).

def: s.f. A dialec. or corr. form of koṭhī, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ā), A frog; an insect, a worm.

ety: koṭ, q.v.+dim. aff. lā = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A small fortress, &c.; — a place where the property of a temple is kept, and its affairs are managed.
n. m. Dim. of koṭ . 1. A fortress. 2. A place where the property of a temple is kept, and its affairs are managed; a chapter.
s.f. (dialec.) = kothlī, and koṭhrī, qq.v. s.f. (dim.) A small bag; a purse, &c. ( = kothlā); — a bag of sweetmeats (sent as a present).
s.m. = kothmīr, q.v. s.f.m.(?) Coriander plant and seed, Coriandrum sativum (cf. dhaniyā).
s. f. A kind of greens, coriander.

s.m. A fort, castle, stronghold; a redoubt; wall of a fort or a city, rampart; entrenchment; a mud bank round a field or village; a magic circle traced with sand or ashes; (met.) the body; — crookedness, curving or crossing of the legs (in sitting)
subs: To sit (tailor-fashion) with the legs crossed; to sit at ease.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑁄𑀝𑁆𑀝 (koṭṭa), from Sanskrit कोट्ट (koṭṭa), कोट (koṭa).

def: = H کوٿوار कोटवार koṭwār, s.m. corr. of کوٿوال kotwāl, q.v.
( = S. कोट or कोष्ठ + पाल), s.m. lit. 'The keeper or porter of a castle'; the chief officer of the police for a city or town; city magistrate.
more: n. m. The chief officer of police for a city or town.
s. m. The chief officer of the police for a city or town.

def: s.f. The status, and the office, of a kotwāl; — the place of business of a kotwāl, the chief police station in a town
more: n. f. The chief police station in a town. kotvālī chaṛhnā , v. a. To bring a criminal action.
s. f. The office of a kotwāl, q. v. کوتوالي چبوتره kotwālī-chabūtara, s. m. A place or hall for the office of a kotwāl.
subs: s.m. A place or hall for the office of a kotwāl.

def: s.m. corr. of next, q.v.
= H کوٿوال कोटवाल koṭwāl, s.m. corr. of کوٿوال kotwāl, q.v.
(in Braj ) Any, any one, some, somebody. See S̱. کوٴي koʼī.
Braj forms of the pron. koʼī, q.v.
s.f. A dialec. form of khaprā or khaprī, 'a tile,' q.v. (other forms are kaul, kaulī).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Knowing, experienced, skilled, learned, wise; — an experienced or a learned man, a panḍit.
s.m. The tree Bauhinia variegata, and its wood, a species of ebony; — one of the trees of paradise.

ety: S. कोष+कः, prob. through a form kohā
def: s.m. Ball (of the eye); corner (of the eye); — the pulp of the jack-fruit, Artocarpus integrifolia; a slice, or division of the jack-fruit; — cocoon (of the silkworm).
s.m. = کويا koyā, q.v. s.m. Ball (of the eye); corner (of the eye); — the pulp of the jack-fruit, Artocarpus integrifolia; a slice, or division of the jack-fruit; — cocoon (of the silkworm).
more: n. m. S. कोण 1. Corner of the eye. 2. The eye of the jack fruit, etc. 3. The cocoon of the silkworm.

def: s.m. Corner, angle ( = koṇ, q.v.).
s.m. A saw.

s.m. A step; a degree; a foot; going, proceeding; passing; a position taken (by an animal, or an assailant) before making a spring or attacking; a bound; — uninterrupted or regular progress, order, succession, series, regular arrangement, sequence, course, way, method, manner; undertaking, enterprise, intention; a sacred precept; power, strength
subs: or kram-kram-se, or krame-krame, adv. Gradually, little by little, by degrees, in course (of, -ke), in due course; in order
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَرَم (karam), from Arabic كَرَم.
